observations.recommendation: Create a record of all actual market stallholders; execute new contracts with stallholders as prior contracts expired.
observations.managementAction:
The management commented that it has completed the bidding for the Construction of a New Two (2) Storey Public Market and they can properly comply with the audit observations during and after the completion of the New Public Market as follows;

Updating the Application of Lease.

The application for lease shall be made under oath and shall be submitted to the office of the treasury by the applicant either in person or his attorney.

Compliance with Section 5A.06 of the Municipal Revenue Code of the LGU.

The contract of lease of stall shall be for the period of one (1) year as minimum and five (5) years a maximum, renewable upon its expiration, upon agreement of both parties, unless revoked under the provision of this article. 

Creation of a new record of all actual market stallholders with their names, period of occupancy, and the monthly rate paid based on the actual mapping of the Market Supervisor for transparency, effective monitoring, and better management of market stalls. 

Updating the Municipal Revenue Code for the new public market.

observations.recommendation: Issue EO to set monthly rental payment for certain buildings; require SB clarification on escalation clause; apply escalation clause; create complete stall records; enforce collection policies; issue Notice of Vacancy; post Lessee and Stall number; close stalls of delinquents; require business permits; SB to consider recommendations in revised Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: Management discussed the operational costs incurred by the current public market and agreed that the non-implementation of various provisions of the revenue code as well as the non-updating thereof contribute to the minimal income derived from its operations. Management assures that the observed lapses will be taken into consideration for the operation of the new public market to be constructed.

observations.recommendation: Implement sanctions against violators of sub-leasing; implement monitoring and inspections; issue public notice of current rental rates; SB to expedite review of Revised Revenue Code and include limit on number of stalls per registrant; SB to consider separate Market Code before operation of New Public Market.
observations.managementAction: Management expressed that they are aware of the sub-leasing of stalls happening in the current public market. They agreed to ensure that list of actual stallholders be updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary corrections and explanations to the Financial Statements and their pertinent Notes, including the required schedules, and submit the revised copy to COA.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to explain the discrepancies noted in the SCBAA and reconciliation statement, and prepare corrected reports. Furthermore, a thorough review and reconciliation should be conducted to ensure accuracy and compliance with IPSAS I and 24.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year 2025, and ensure that the document is provided on time at the beginning of each year.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to finalize the review of the list of uncollected RPT Receivables as of December 31, 2024, considering all advance payments made by the taxpayers in prior years to properly adjust the beginning balance of the RPT and SET Receivables. Submit the Journal Entry Voucher to support the adjusting entry made.
Require the Municipal Accountant to address the noted errors listed in the observation and ensure that all collections, discounts, and penalties are accurately recorded.
Require the Treasury and the Accounting Office to perform regular reconciliation to ensure all reciprocal accounts are balanced and adequately supported.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a separate record of all advance payments, and for the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate Subsidiary Ledgers of Deferred RPT and SET accounts for all advance payments.
Require the Municipal Accountant to use the following supporting documents and computations as the basis for the ending balances of the accounts:

We recommended that Management:
Require Municipal Accountant to adjust the inventory accounts using the proposed entry:
Prior Period Adjustment					₱3,660,385.00
Office Supplies and Materials Inventory 				₱41,760.00
ICT Equipment								₱41,760.00
Require the General Services Officer (GSO) to ensure the proper filing of Inventory Custodian Slips for issued semi-expendable properties and to promptly inform the Municipal Accountant of such issuances to facilitate the proper recognition of expense accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Office of the Municipal Engineer and the Project Monitoring and Evaluation Committee to furnish the Office of the Municipal Accountant with copies of the verified Statement of Work Accomplished and the Project Monitoring Reports (PMR) for all completed and ongoing projects without billing as of year-end.
Adopt the accrual basis of accounting by recording ongoing projects at year-end based on work accomplished or the percentage of completion, as indicated in the Report on GPPA, regardless of when payment is made.
Ensure that the Report on GPPA at year-end is supported by verified SWA or PMR; and
Make the necessary adjusting entries to record the three ongoing projects and two completed projects by debiting the appropriate asset accounts and crediting the Accounts Payable account in the relevant General and Trust Fund ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare correcting entries to record bank charges and commitment fees as follows:
Prior Period Adjustment	₱778,775.00
Loans Payable		       ₱778,775.00
Ensure that the following accounts have 2024 restated balances as follows:
Loans Payable			₱ 68,661,428.60
Bank Charges			₱696,425.00
Commitment Fees			₱82,350.00

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to study the discrepancies presented in this observation and make the necessary adjustments in both reports, as applicable.

We recommended that Management:
Use the appropriate journal entry to record the Petty Cash replenishment in the Check Disbursements Journal.
Prepare an Excel file as a manual cash disbursements journal to record the Petty Cash Vouchers considering the accounting system limitations mentioned. This will serve as a supplementary record that supports the account titles and amounts used in the Journal Entry Voucher during replenishments.

We recommended that Management:
a. Designate a special disbursing officer responsible under the Municipal Treasury Office for the payment of the financial assistance and subsequent liquidation thereof. This is without prejudice with Management’s installation of other internal control measures that would adhere to the prescribed policy; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the cash advance for payments of AICS using the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account whenever payment is made. Further, ensure that these cash advances are liquidated accordingly, with complete supporting documents and through the use of Disbursement Vouchers instead of Petty Cash Vouchers.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to comply with Sections 114, 121, and 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, particularly on the preparation of various documents that support the issuance and the ending balance reported in the Financial Statements.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Supply Ledger Cards (SLC) for each commodity or stock, and the GSO to prepare stock cards and property cards for supplies.
Require the various offices of the LGU to compile monitoring reports on the receipt and issuance of supplies and to regularly provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant with these reports.
Require the Municipal Accountant to explain the noted deficiencies for each inventory account and provide any adjustments made, if applicable.

We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that the delivery terms and conditions in the Bidding Documents, along with the contract amount and date of approval in the Contract Agreement and Purchase Order, are consistent to avoid confusion during implementation.
b. Charge the supplier the computed Liquidated Damages.
c. Adjust the books to cancel the recording of 2,000 units received only in 2025.
d. Ensure that the distribution of fertilizers to beneficiaries is supported by a master list duly signed by each farmer and showing the date of actual receipt.
e. Require the Office of Municipal Agriculture to submit distribution documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely recording of adjustments to the Inventory and Expense Accounts.

We recommended that Management through its Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries:
a. Transfer the remaining LGSF funds and Due to NGA account to the Trust Fund books.

To adjust the balance of government equity to reflect the actual amount of funds utilized.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Budget and the Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for QRF, for MOOE, and for CO related to LDRRMF and regularly update the same when supplemental budgets and SB resolutions are issued, and whenever disbursements are prepared.
Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the amount of unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund transferred to Trust Fund in the 2025 journals as follows:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the appropriate cash in bank balance from Trust Fund account to General Fund account. This ensures that the funds are properly allocated and accounted for.
Require the Municipal Accountant to use proper account to transfer unexpended LDRRM Fund of current year appropriations.
Require the MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Office to review the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF before submission to the Local Auditor, Office of the Civil Defense and Department of the Interior and Local Government for added control on the correctness of amounts.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the amount of Trust Liabilities - LDRRM Fund for CY 2023 in the 2025 journals as follows:
Disburse funds from the Trust Fund in case of utilization of unexpended LDRRM Fund from prior years within the next five (5) years.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly prohibit the use of the LDRRM Fund for peace and order activities and for any LGU health and wellness programs. This is a direct violation of its intended purpose and must be prevented to avoid the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.
Require the MDRRMO, MPDC, and Municipal Budget Office to adequately plan, evaluate, and thoroughly assess the projects, projects and activities included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan. They must ensure that each program, project, and activity align with disaster risk reduction and Management activities.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit breakdown of land items to support the recorded balance of ₱40,489,750.00.
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct inventory of properties of the LGU, especially the Land Accounts and prepare updated report of its condition, and location.
Instruct the Municipal Assessor to exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership on untitled lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title in the name of the Municipal Government of Manaoag pursuant to Section 39 of PD No. 1445 in order to protect the interest of the LGU for any adverse claims by third parties.

We recommended that Management:
Implement the guidelines and procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPEs of the LGU per COA Circular 2020-006.
Require the Property Officer, to prepare and submit RPCPPE or Physical Inventory Report and for Municipal Accountant to prepare and update PPE Ledger Cards as part of the preliminary activities.
Submit documents related to disposal of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that the procurement is in accordance with the Annual Procurement Plan prior to advertisement or the issuance of the Invitation to Bid.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the LSB to follow the timeline of activities in order to facilitate timely approval of SEF plan and budget.
Direct the LSB to provide a copy of the approved SEF Budget to the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer within seven days following the approval of the SEF Budget;
Require the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to indicate the date of receipt to assess the timeliness of the LSB's submission.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization based on the required timeline and furnish copies to the LSB.
Direct the LSB to submit quarterly and annual reports to the DepEd CO within the prescribed deadlines, with copies provided to the local sanggunian and the Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.

We recommend that the Management:
Require the Local School Board to include only the allowed expenditure items enumerated in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars No. 1 series of 2017 and 2020 in the SEF Budget.
Require the Local School Board to describe the project, program and activity per school in the SEF budget with enough specifications to avoid vague budget items.
Require the Local School Board to include ECCD programs in the SEF budget.
Refrain from issuing personal checks to disburse funds from Special Education Fund.
Require the payees enumerated in Annex A of the issued AOM to submit the noted deficiency in liquidation documents.
Require the payees from Table 1 and 2 and Annex A of the issued AOM to submit certification from DepEd that the expenditures were not reimbursed by the payees.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to exercise caution in preparing the checks for disbursement based on OBR to avoid improper charging from General Fund.
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit proof of adjustment for the improper charging of funds from general fund.

We recommended that Management –
a. Require the contractor, in writing, to immediately resume work on the project. If the contractor fails to comply, proceed with contract termination as outlined in Annex I of the IRR for RA 9184;
b. Compute the liquidated damages incurred by the Contractor upon completion of the project, prior to the release of the final payment, and deduct it from the project cost.
c. Issue Suspension Orders only for valid reasons enumerated in paragraph 10, Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184.
d. Use the format provided by DPWH Department Order No. 42, Series of 2020 for suspension orders, resumption orders and extension orders to be issued by Management for increased monitoring of remaining contract days.

We recommend that the Management:
a) Conduct detailed engineering activities and prepare report thereof for every infrastructure project before bidding and awarding of contracts. This include site assessments, and verification of land ownership to ensure that all project requirements and legal conditions are met.
b) Ensure that the Deed of Donation for the lot related to the project is fully executed before resuming construction works. The Deed of Donation must be verified and secured in order to protect public funds and ensure the project serves its intended purpose for the community.

We recommend that Management:
a. Terminate the contract for the Construction of Pathway at the Church Perimeter, with a total cost of ₱4,470,865.23.
b. Require the Municipal Engineering Office to impose liquidated damages on the contractor due to the delayed completion of works from April 21, 2022, to the present, as no termination of contract has been made.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the lacking documentations noted in the observation.
Require the same to submit a written explanation on each of the deficiencies discussed in subsections (a) to (d).
Discuss the observed issues and lapses with the Bids and Awards Committee, Design and Build Committee, and Technical Working Group and identify areas where responsibilities may not have been fully met by each member.
Require the BAC to revisit the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 to enhance their understanding of the procurement process and help avoid similar issues in the future.

We recommended that Management:
Grant monetization of leave credits of employees if at least 5 days of vacation leave is retained after monetization;
Ensure the strict monitoring and updating of leave credits to prevent negative balance of leave credits of any employee and facilitate adjustment to compensation once undertimes, tardiness and/or absences was made by employees without any leave credits balance.
For employees with a negative balance, HRMO should only allow leave without pay and should not allow them to monetize their leave credits until they have earned enough credits and have a minimum balance of five days in their leave ledger card.

We recommended that Management:
Provide a report of JO workers who had amounts deducted from the January 2025 payroll for days without actual work in December, as informed by the HRMO. In the absence of the said document, immediate deductions shall be made to recoup the overpaid amounts.
Ensure that the Time Book and Payroll, Daily Time Record, and Accomplishment Reports have a consistent number of actual workdays before processing wage payments.

We recommended that Management:
Provide explanations or clarifications on each of the noted observations.
Provide the lacking documents noted in subsections (7) to (12).
Grant financial assistance to PNP through their official bank account for increased transparency and accountability.
Reconsider the amount of financial assistance given for Zumba fitness sessions in the Municipality and require additional documentation as to participation to evaluate the number of constituents benefited by their services.
Require only the intake sheet from MSWD Office for the financial assistance charged from AICS.
Ensure that the delivery terms posted in PhilGEPS and the Purchase Orders are consistent.

We recommended that Finance Management stop the use of reimbursements for procurements such as meals, supplies, repairs and other disbursements, and instead process payments directly to suppliers through checks, in line with Section 2 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Exceptions may be allowed only for urgent or petty expenses where check issuance is impractical, provided that proper documentation and justification are submitted to support the necessity of such reimbursements and prevent their misuse as a regular procurement method.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer the immediate submission of the vouchers listed in Annex B of the issued AOM.
Evaluate the manpower complement in the Accounting Office and appropriate funds for the cost of additional staff, particularly to aid in the sorting of vouchers to avoid undue delays.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to set deadlines to LGU personnel on the claiming of wages and salaries to ensure timely liquidation of payroll.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the BPLO, to devise measures to validate the declared gross receipts of establishments applying for permits to engage in business, especially those with direct transactions with the LGU and its barangays.
Enforce the collection of underpayments, including the applicable surcharges and interest as provided in the Municipal Revenue Code. Non-compliance may necessitate the issuance of a corresponding Notice of Charge.
Refrain from issuing Mayor’s Permit to and entering into contract with noted LGU suppliers with unpaid tax obligations.
Include in the Instruction to Bidders or other documents issued by the Bids and Awards Committee that prospective suppliers/bidders should have no unpaid obligation with the Municipality as an eligibility requirement. A certification by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant must be issued to effect this requirement.
Submit the approved application for a business permit for one more LGU supplier, Larry Auto Repair Shop.

We recommended that Management:
Request a new electrical post from Dagupan Electric Cooperative and the relocation of the live wire immediately to ensure safety and before any additional construction on the structure.
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that Detailed Engineering activities have been conducted before bidding and awarding of contracts to prevent idle projects in the LGU due to non-usability of design.
Require the Municipal Planning Development Council, in cooperation with the Office of the Municipal Engineer, to ensure that all future infrastructure projects with multiple phases are included in the Annual Investment Plan until their completion.

We recommended that Management:
Submit timeline of actions to be undertaken to put each of the idle facilities to work and operate.
Issue a Municipal Ordinance on waste segregation to facilitate easier collection of residual wastes.
Require the Municipal Planning Development Council to submit the initial clearance from DOH, Site Development Plan, Manual on Operations and Revised Revenue Code, if any, containing the operation of the new Public Cemetery.
Inquire from experts to arrive at an informed decision whether or not to maintain or dispose the laboratory machines taking into consideration efficiency and effectivity in its use.
Submit clearance from DOH to operate X-ray and Ultrasound machines in the Super RHU.
The MPDO and Tourism offices should lead in planning how to encourage the occupancy of vendors in the Municipal Covered Food Court. Surely any attraction that would drive consumers to gather within the vicinity of this structure is enough reason for sellers to follow suit.  Further, submit proof of installation of water faucets and light bulbs to COA. Otherwise, justify, the non-utilization of the facilities.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the inventory account and prior period adjustment with necessary documentations;

Ensure the prompt and accurate communication by all end-users of any inventory consumption or distribution to the Office of the Municipal Accountant. This should be done by providing the necessary documentation, enabling the latter to prepare timely and correct adjusting entries.

Require the Office of the Municipal Engineer to submit copies of Statement of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, Suspension and Resumption Orders related to the noted projects. 

Require the Office of the Municipal Engineer to establish and define clear timelines to contractors for project completion notification. Ensure that contractors understand the expected timeframe for submitting notifications. Also, emphasize the importance of timely communication for efficient project management.

observations.managementAction:
Management submitted Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) indicating 100% completion as of November 16, 2023 for Improvement of Motorpool Extension which was received by the LGU only on February 2024.

While Management informed that the Construction of Barangay Road Network FMR Sitio Pugaro, Brgy. Tebuel amounting to ₱498,535.45 was cancelled.

They also informed that the project Concreting the Barangay Road Network, Network Irrigation Road, Matulong amounting to ₱499,877.45 is already completed and even submitted Journal Entry Voucher for the adjustment of Advances to contractors at ₱74,981.61

Management also admitted that in order to ensure that certain projects will be classified as continuing appropriations, these are classified as Capital Outlay regardless of the short-term and operating expense nature of the project such as repairs and maintenance.

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct inventory of LGU properties, with a particular focus on the Land account. Instruct them to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Land and Land Improvements to ascertain the existence, precise location, description, and condition of these lots.

Likewise, Report on the Physical Count on Land and Land Improvements will be submitted once ascertained.

Management submitted Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) indicating 100% completion as of November 16, 2023 for Improvement of Motorpool Extension which was received by the LGU only on February 2024.
While Management informed that the Construction of Barangay Road Network FMR Sitio Pugaro, Brgy. Tebuel amounting to ₱498,535.45 was cancelled.
They also informed that the project Concreting the Barangay Road Network, Network Irrigation Road, Matulong amounting to ₱499,877.45 is already completed and even submitted Journal Entry Voucher for the adjustment of Advances to contractors at ₱74,981.61

Management believes that the full implementation of the said construction projects would bring more benefits to their constituents. Rest assured the full utilization of the same to fully harness the benefits of the planned objectives of the projects.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Despite the Audit Team’s recommendation to submit copies of Statement of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, Suspension and Resumption Orders related to the noted projects, Management did not provide the required documentations.
For the Improvement of Motorpool (Extension), the GPPA report showed that the project started on August 16, 2023 with target completion date on October 8, 2023. However, the SWA was as of November 16, 2023 which was beyond the target completion date. No suspension or resume order was submitted.
The Audit Team also recommended to require the contractors to submit explanation for delayed request for billing but none was submitted.
There was no document to support the cancellation of this project such as notice of cancellation of bidding, or notice of termination of contract of Construction of Barangay Road Network FMR Sitio Pugaro, Brgy. Tebuel.
The audit team would like to clarify that as of December 31, 2023, the LGU maintains two advances to contractors related to Matulong FMR projects:
Date               Amount            Contract Cost
Dec. 13, 2021    ₱74,982.37         ₱499,882.47
May 5, 2022      ₱74,981.61         ₱499,877.45
Inspection of the documents attached to DV no. 111-23-05-1175 on May 18, 2023 for the payment of 100% completion of Concreting of Barangay Road Municipal Wide along Matulong Road Network, Barangay Matulong, Manaoag at ₱496,806.17 revealed that the contracts and other supporting documents were related to the project with original contract cost of ₱499,882.47 but with revised contract cost due to variation order of ₱496,806.17. Thus, the advances to contractors that must be adjusted related to this project was ₱74,982.37 from the Trust Fund, instead of the ₱74,981.61.
However, we also note that included in the documents attached to DV No. 111-23-05-1175 is the  geo-tagged photos of the project by contractor Alphin Trading with indicated project allocation at ₱499,877.45. This inclusion would then be inconsistent with other documents which relates to ₱499,882.47 project.
Thus, without submitting SWA, inspection reports and other documents related to the project with contract cost of ₱499,877.45, actual status of completion and billing remains uncertain.

Refrain from incurring payments for meals and snacks of personnel rendering regular duties or not included in official meetings.
Ensure that representation expenses incurred are duly supported by complete documentations to validate claims.
During exit conference, the members of the BAC agreed to refrain from charging the payments for meals and snacks during bidding activities from the LGU Fund. Management also informed that meals and snacks given to security and other unnamed personnel were already discontinued. There were no comments given on other noted observations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to review the computation provided in subsection (1) and (2) and require the concerned payees to refund any excess travel claims after verification.
Create internal guidelines on the rate of per diems to be claimed by employees for travels within the 50-km radius, ensuring uniformity and reasonableness.
Refrain from allowing claims of per diems and fare exceeding the rates allowed by EO 77.
Consider including provisions for driver’s claims in the internal guidelines to ensure that reasonable travel claims are allowed.
Ensure that proper account titles are used to record transactions.
During exit conference, Management agreed to creating internal guidelines which includes matrix of allowable claims for uniformity.

We recommended that Management:
Justify the granting of financial assistance for each of the noted observations.
Verify the grant and liquidation of cash advances for travel claims of the payees in subsection (4) and assess the need to refund the financial assistance given by the LGU.
Establish a systematic evaluation process for assessing and approving financial assistance, ensuring that sufficient documentations are required.
During exit conference, Management informed that contracts will already be secured with the fitness instructors as basis for the payments of their services instead of as financial assistance. Management also explained that payments to CDSOG are for their assistance in traffic management and assured that proper documentations will be prepared in the future. On financial assistance to PB AAA in subsection (4), Management explained that the payee requested in behalf of all other Punong Barangays. On financial assistance given through petty cash fund, Management discussed about simplifying the documents Case Evaluation Report/Intake Sheets usually prepared by MSWD, in order to support payments through the PCF.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure properly accomplished and duly approved trip ticket is secured by the driver before using government vehicles.
Explain the use of various plate numbers not included in the list of municipal vehicles.
Require the Property Officer to conduct inventory of all Municipal Vehicles to update the list, ensure that all vehicles are duly registered, and assign proper identification for use by drivers in their accomplishment of trip tickets.
During exit conference, Management explained that LGU receives request for gas augmentation from other departments such as fire and police which may explain the various plate numbers indicated in the gas slips.

We recommended Management the following:
To prepare updated list of unserviceable properties which will serve as a documentation to provide clear records and existence and condition of these properties.
To submit appraisal reports from the in-house or independent appraiser to verify accuracy and fairness of the property valuation.
To request an inspection from COA for the unserviceable properties earmarked for disposal to verify the condition of the properties.
To prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to facilitate the disposal and dropping from the books of the unserviceable properties, and to undertake the necessary actions for their disposal in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386.
The Municipal Treasurers Office has already started gathering reports for the unserviceable properties in every office for them to prepare the inventory and inspection report for unserviceable property to facilitate the disposal and dropping from the books of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Cash Disbursements Journal for all Petty Cash Fund transactions in CY 2024.
During exit conference, Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Submit justification for the delayed and non- submission of the documents for CY 2022-2023 projects.
Strictly comply in the submission of copies of perfected contracts, approved POs, and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period of five (5) working days as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 for CY 2024 projects. Administrative disciplinary action as provided in Section 4 of the same Circular against the concerned employee be imposed upon unjustified failure of compliance.
Management commented it has issued a memorandum to Head of the BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant, to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-01, to submit perfected contracts to COA Team Leader within five (5) working days and to strictly follow the required timeliness of the Procurement Activities under 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to avoid delays.
Management requested for exclusion of BAC Chairperson from the list of persons determined as liable/responsible under subject AOM on the reasons that Bids and Awards Committee duties and responsibilities were only up to awards of contracts under 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184

We recommended that Management:
Evaluate the manpower complement in the HRMO and Accounting office and consider the hiring of additional staff, particularly to aid in the preparation and attachment of DTR and sorting of vouchers, respectively, to avoid undue delays.
Direct the HRMO to set deadlines on submission of DTRs and accomplishment reports with the provision to exclude from payroll those individuals lacking required documents prior to preparation of disbursement vouchers.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to stop releasing checks without complete signatories on vouchers or disbursing cash without signatures on payroll and other documents.  Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant not to record the liquidation of transactions without complete signatories on vouchers and other attachments.
Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all unsubmitted vouchers for CY 2023.
Management commented that it will be requiring the staff to diligently check the completeness of the signatures in the DVs before preparing checks. Likewise, it will ensure the signatures and submission of ORs /ARs when claiming checks.
It will also enforce the non-releasing of cash without complete signatures especially in the issuance of Financial Assistance & for the items in the MOOE.
More so, it will remind the staff to submit payroll within 3 to 4 days to the Accounting Office with complete signatures and also attach SPA or Authorizations, if there are any, in order to avoid delay.
It will also remind all staffs on the submission of DVs & Payrolls to the Accounting Office on or before the 5th day of the following month to avoid delay, and to ensure submission to the COA within the 10th day of the following month. Lastly, for the staff of the Treasury handling cash, they shall ensure submission of DVs & Payrolls to the Accounting Office of every after replenishment.

We recommended Management, thru its Sanggunian Bayan, to ensure expeditious processing of the review and approval of the revised revenue code that will yield new/additional sources of revenues that will support the programs/projects/activities of the municipality advantageous for the members of the community.
The management commented that they agree with the observations on the missed opportunities due to non-passage of the updated Revenue Code. They said that the passage of the same now rests with the Sangguniang Bayan, as they are the appropriate body to enact laws and ordinances. The Treasury Office could only remind them to expedite the processing, review and approval of the updated Revenue Code since the Executive and Legislative are two different bodies.

We recommended that Management:
Resolve any matters with the landowner to facilitate completion of the road construction leading to the Sanitary Landfill, ensuring its accessibility.
Require the Municipal Planning Development Council to expedite the preparation and finalization of processing of documentations (e.g. site development plan, manual of operations and updated revenue code) necessary in the operation of the Public Cemetery.
Submit plans detailing further courses of action to be taken in the usage of the X-Ray, Doppler Ultrasonic System and Hemodialysis machine in order to assess Management that funds used to procure were efficiently and effectively utilized.
Devise ways to entice vendors to sell in the Municipal Covered Food Court to be able to serve its intended purpose.
Submit proof of installation of water faucets and light bulbs in the Food Court once operational.
Facilitate monitoring, assign Department Heads as dedicated focal persons in the different undertakings with clear timelines or schedules, outlining the steps to be undertaken to put each of the facilities to work and operate.
Management commented the following:
It has coordinated with the Barangay Council of Pugaro to subdue the landowner’s relative in order to complete the projects. To date, the contractor has already progressed with the construction.
It has coordinated with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan to also expedite the approval of the Updated Revenue Code. Likewise, they are already finalizing the necessary documents for the approval of the Municipal Memorial Park.
They will be submitting a separate action plan for the utilization/transfer of medical equipment in the Super RHU.
The Provincial Government of Pangasinan intends to develop a Multi-level parking at the back of Manaoag Central School and an access road to the church from the parking area. These projects will benefit the vendors who will be selling at the Food Court in the long-run. These collaboarative projects will entice the vendors to sell in the Municipal Covered Food Court.
They will submit proof of installation of water faucets and light bulbs in the Food Court once operational.
Various Department Heads to facilitate immediate utilization of the said facilities were assigned.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to create a record of all actual market stallholders with their names, period of occupancy, and monthly rate paid based on the actual mapping of the Market Supervisor. This will help promote transparency, effective monitoring and better management of market stalls.
Execute a new contract with the actual stallholders, as the former contracts have already expired after 5 years, to establish the legality of the market stallholders’ occupancy and to ensure the enforceability of claims or rights in the event of a breach of provisions as stated in the Revenue Code.
The management commented that it has completed the bidding for the Construction of a New Two (2) Storey Public Market and they can properly comply with the audit observations during and after the completion of the New Public Market as follows;
Updating the Application of Lease.
The application for lease shall be made under oath and shall be submitted to the office of the treasury by the applicant either in person or his attorney.
Compliance with Section 5A.06 of the Municipal Revenue Code of the LGU.
The contract of lease of stall shall be for the period of one (1) year as minimum and five (5) years a maximum, renewable upon its expiration, upon agreement of both parties, unless revoked under the provision of this article.
Creation of a new record of all actual market stallholders with their names, period of occupancy, and the monthly rate paid based on the actual mapping of the Market Supervisor for transparency, effective monitoring, and better management of market stalls.
Updating the Municipal Revenue Code for the new public market.

We recommended that the Management:
Issue an Executive Order to formally set the monthly rental payment of stalls for Building numbers 10 to 18, based on the recommendation of the Municipal Treasurer or the Market Supervisor, subject to change upon the effectivity of the revised Revenue Code being reviewed by the Sangguniang Bayan.
Require the Sangguniang Bayan to issue a clarification on the implementation of the escalation clause stated in paragraph (b) of section 5A.05.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to apply the escalation clause on monthly rental payments based on the clarification of the SB.
Require the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Market Supervisor to create a complete record of stalls to monitor payments of the stall owners.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of monthly rentals every 10th of the month and impose 25% penalty charges for late payments.
Issue Notice of Vacancy, as provided in paragraph (b) of Section 5A.06 of the Revenue Code, for stalls with no business activity such as those used for deposit or warehouse of goods, to offer the public an opportunity to apply for lease.
Require the Market Supervisor to post the name of Lessee and Stall number in conspicuous place in front of each stall.
Enforce the closing of stalls of delinquent stall owners based on the record of payments from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.
Require the stall owners to secure business permits and applicable licenses from the LGU.
Require the Sangguniang Bayan to consider the observations and recommendations stated herein in their review of the revised Municipal Revenue Code.
During exit conference, Management discussed the operational costs incurred by the current public market and agreed that the non-implementation of various provisions of the revenue code as well as the non-updating thereof contribute to the minimal income derived from its operations. Management assures that the observed lapses will be taken into consideration for the operation of the new public market to be constructed.

We recommended the following:
For the Management to strictly implement applicable sanctions as provided in Chapter V Section 5A.05 paragraph (l) of the Municipal Revenue Code and Section 5A. 10 of the same Code, against stall owners identified to be involved in violating the abovementioned provision.
In order to mitigate or discourage sub-leasing practices, Management is encouraged to implement thorough monitoring procedures, regular conduct of inspections and raise awareness among stall owners regarding the potential consequences associated with sub-leasing.
Management should consider issuing an order to inform the public of the actual current rate of rent of the market stalls, subject to change pending the issuance of the revised Revenue Code. This communication will help keep the public informed and aware of the current situation, while also preparing them for potential changes in the future.
For the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan to expedite the review of the Revised Revenue Code and include specific provision that define and limit the number of stall one can register, promoting a more organized and fair market environment.
For the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan to consider issuance of a separate Market Code of the Municipality before the operation of the New Public Market which is yet to be constructed, to establish a fresh set of rules and regulations specifically governing market operations taking into consideration the guidelines on stall allocation, leasing procedures and other relevant aspects as well as to the lapses noted in issued AOMs.
During exit conference, Management expressed that they are aware of the sub-leasing of stalls happening in the current public market. They agreed to ensure that list of actual stallholders be updated.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant has to reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer relative to the amount of delinquent local taxes and disclosed the correct amount in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already instructed to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers.

Direct the Market Supervisor to regularly prepare the monthly billing statements for the delinquent stallholders and regularly submit a summary of the delinquent stallholders to the Municipal Treasurer and to furnish the Municipal Accountant, who will then take up the unpaid balances as a debit to Operating Lease Receivable and credit to Rent Income; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere with the accrual method of accounting by properly recognizing the uncollected balances from the delinquent stallholders, as prescribed under Paragraph 19 of IPSAS No. 9 and pertinent provision of GAM for LGUs.

Ensure that a physical inventory be conducted on inventory items at least once a year and the result of the physical count be reported in the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) which shall be submitted to the concerned Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

For other concerns on supply management and accounting, Management should strictly adhere to and be guided by Section 175 to 207 of Volume I of Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

The balance of the PPE general ledger accounts should be reconciled with subsidiary ledger cards and other property records like Property Acknowledgment Receipts in compliance with Section 269, Volume I of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the aforementioned supporting documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team. Strict compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 by not allowing payment for claims with incomplete supporting papers is further recommended.

The Municipal Treasurer already instructed to be more prudent in procuring electronic devices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management limit the hiring of experts to its actual need of consultancy services taking into consideration the specific work requiring highly specialized or technical expertise in a field of special knowledge or training which cannot be provided by the regular staff of the Municipality. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of monthly accomplishment report from the Consultants to support the claim of honorarium.

Management submitted an Executive Order authorizing the transfer of fund from the vacant positions in the plantilla to Honorarium account both under the Office of the Municipal Mayor and under Personnel Services (subject to PS limitation).

observations.recommendation:
To strengthen the internal control in the use of government vehicles and to regulate fuel consumption of the Municipality, we recommended that at the end of every month, the agency submit to the COA Audit Team Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Official Fuel Consumption for audit purposes as required in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. 

In addition, the following measures are hereby recommended:

For motor vehicle which is not exclusively used by any government official, it is recommended that each time the vehicle is used, require that the vehicle be returned  in the office  full tank, thus the actual no. of liters consumed for each travel is immediately determined and the same shall be indicated in the Driver’s Trip Ticket; and

Ensure that all Driver’s Trip Ticket are properly numbered and recorded in a logbook for control purposes and maintain a separate file of the Driver’s Trip Ticket.

It is further recommended that policies and guidelines as clearly defined under COA Circular No. 77-61 be given due regard for proper control and monitoring of vehicle utilization and gasoline consumption.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to –

Accept receipt in accordance with BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 as evidence of payment in the procurement of goods; and

Submit the Sales Invoice pertaining to the aforementioned transactions to support the payments made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management release the share of the barangays from the basic real property tax earnings on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with the provision of Section 271 of RA No. 7160 also known and cited as the Local Government Code of 1991 to enable the barangays to use the additional income for development projects which are beneficial to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was already instructed to release the share of the barangays from basic real property tax earnings within the prescribed period.

Ensure that all accounts and reports for succeeding months be submitted on or before the 10th day of the following month to the Auditor pursuant to Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-06 to enable the conduct of timely audit of transactions and to ensure validity, completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office was already instructed to strictly observe the submission of disbursement vouchers, payrolls and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

For long outstanding delinquent accounts, Management may consider to impose civil remedies as provided under Section 174, Chapter IV of R.A. 7160 to enforce and intensify the collection of these accounts in order to generate additional income for the financial operation of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the projects/programs/activities designed under the 20% Development Fund are implemented fully within the time frame. Optimal utilization of the 20% IRA is also recommended in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents towards the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all.
observations.managementAction: Construction of awarded projects from the 20% Development Fund is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management –

Stop from charging expenses not related in addressing gender issues and promoting women’s empowerment and gender equality as provided in the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01; and

Require the GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer to submit explanation/s on why the amount of above-mentioned expenditures reflected in the GAD Accomplishment Report exceeded the amount of appropriations provided therefor.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer explained the GAD expenditures which exceeded the amount incorporated in the GAD plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairman of the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC), implement programs, projects and activities (PPAs) under LCPC in accordance with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 to address the needs of children and other related issues that need immediate attention and action. 

We also recommended that members of the LCPC maximize the allocated LCPC Fund to identified PPAs for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries to fully achieve the objectives of the law creating the fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 27, page 98

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in setting-up of receivables for real property taxes and special education taxes at the beginning of the year for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

Management expressed its willingness to comply with the given recommendations.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the guidelines relative to the monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when become due and demandable. Strict compliance with the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 is further recommended to avoid overstatement of LGU’s assets due to inclusion of accounts with doubtful existence for the fair presentation of receivable accounts in the financial statements.

Management commented that the Municipal Accountant was already instructed to start the procedure in the write-off of dormant accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive –
Instruct the Municipal Assessor to provide this Office a photocopy of land titles of parcels of land to ascertain the legality of LGU’s ownership over these parcels of land;
Exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership on untitled lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipal Government of Manaoag pursuant to Section 39 of PD No. 1445 and Section 449, Volume 1 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual in order to protect the interest of the LGU for any adverse claims by third parties; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds which shall cover the expenses on the registration of parcels of land.

Management commented that relative to the application for Special Patents for the government properties of the Municipality, copies of the issued Special Patents shall be provided to the Audit Team for the following:
Municipal Auditorium
Rizal Park
Municipal Cemetery
Municipal Market (3,000 sq.m.-adjacent to Rizal Street)
Management also informed the Audit Team that the other government properties are now being requested for the issuance of Special Patent.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015. We further recommended the following courses of action:
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system;
Require the Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of General Services Officer, to maintain a Report of the Local Road Network and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
Create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

Management commented that the Local Chief Executive have instructed the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system.

Likewise, Management required the Municipal Treasurer, the designated General Services Officer, to maintain a Report of the Local Road Network and Road Map, and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.

An inventory committee was already created who is conducting the physical count and is preparing the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Ledger Card  and Property Cards, respectively, which are necessary to support the accuracy and reliability of the balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Likewise, to address this perennial issue on doubtful presentation of PPE accounts in the financial statement, we have strongly recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer/Acting Property Officer, the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory/Disposal  Committee to comply strictly with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts  of the LGU in order to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

Management commented that the Local Chief Executive already instructed the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Ledger Card and Property Cards. Likewise, the management is already on the final stage of cleansing of PPE accounts in pursuance to COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to revert back to the General Fund the unexpended balance of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) for Capital Outlay (CO) and Continuing Appropriation in the amount of ₱33,800.00 and ₱750,000.00, respectively, which shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefore are completed and any savings shall be available for use in the disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in LDRRMFIP.

Management commented that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for Capital Outlay and Continuing Appropriation was returned to the General Fund in May 2022.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned Circular and revert back the unexpended balance of Trust Liabilities – DRRMF for CY 2015 from Trust Fund to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund which could be used for other social services of the Municipality.

Management commented that the unexpended balance of Trust Liabilities – DRRMF for CY 2015 was reverted from Trust Fund to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund on April 8, 2022.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correcting entry to transfer the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax from Trust Fund to the General Fund and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that appropriations are provided therefor in the utilization thereof;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the proper accounts prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units for accurate and reliable presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to furnish the Audit Team copy of the list of programs and projects to be implemented supported by the approved Sanggunian Ordinance or resolution, mechanism and period of implementation relative to the Share Tobacco Excise Tax received as required in Sections 2.5, 2.6 and 3.2 of Local Budget Memorandum No. 76 of DBM dated October 6, 2017.

Management expressed its intention to comply with the given recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting or correcting entries and strictly observe the proper accounts prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units for accurate and reliable presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

Management agreed to comply with the given audit recommendations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, to be more prudent in purchasing portable electronic devices which are essential and responsive to the exigency of the service, and see to it that procurement of electronic devices is within the bounds of propriety. Strict compliance with the guidelines on the prevention of unnecessary and extravagant expenditures as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-003 is further recommended;
Require the Tourism Officer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit written justification on the necessity of two (2) laptops in the performance of their respective duties and functions; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Property Acknowledgment Receipts are duly accomplished as to number, date and other vital information.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, to be more prudent in purchasing portable electronic gadgets that are essential and responsive to the exigency of the service, and see to it that the procurement of electronic gadgets is within the bounds of propriety. Strict compliance with the guidelines on the prevention of unnecessary and extravagant expenditures as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-003 is further recommended; and
Require the Sangguniang Bayan Officials, SB Secretary, and SB Staff to submit written justification on the necessity of mobile phones with such specifications in the performance of their respective duties and functions.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of granting Transportation Allowance (TA) to officials and employees with assigned government vehicle/s or are presently using government motor transportation pursuant to Section 8.2.1 of Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.

Management commented that all government vehicles are used as utility vehicles and not for exclusive use of government officials or employees. Nonetheless, Management agreed to comply with the recommendations of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to make payment thru check directly in the name of the supplier or creditor instead of granting cash advances. Strict compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations pertaining to granting and liquidation of cash advances is further recommended.

Management is amenable to the observation and expressed its willingness to comply with the given recommendations.

We recommended to Management the following cours es of action:
Limit the procurement of goods and services on a reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application;

We recommended that Management –
Stop from charging expenses not related in addressing gender issues and promoting women’s empowerment and gender equality as provided in the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01; and
Require the GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer to submit explanation/s on why the amount of above-mentioned expenditures reflected in the GAD Accomplishment Report exceeded the amount of appropriations provided therefor.
Management commented that some of the PPAs of the GAD plan for CY 2020 were attributed/mainstreamed from the item under the MSWD and RHU budgeted PPAs like the Procurement of medicines, Supplemental feeding, Operation of STAC and others. The said PPAs were incorporated in the GAD plans which were submitted to the DILG for their review and approval. Since the DILG issued the Certificate of Review and Endorsement on June 29, 2020, stated that the Gad plan was fully compliant in form and contents, Management assumed that all the PPAs incorporated in the plan were all GAD accepted PPAs as per PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which states that “Programs, Projects, Activities (PPAs) included in the DILG approved LGU-GAD plan can be considered as GAD accepted PPAs.
Management also explained the GAD expenditures which exceeded the amount incorporated in the GAD plan.
1) Conduct of supplemental feeding and distribution of vitamins amounting to ₱1,530,000.00 compared to the ₱1,200,000.00 appropriation as per GAD plan. Of the ₱1,530,000.00 expenditures only ₱32,185.00 was charged to the LGU under the office of the MSWD while the rest was subsidies by the MSWD Regional Office I, and it was given directly to the concern Day Centers.
2) Substitution from home activities to the Day Care Center and monitor growth development of the child amounting to ₱1,530,184.34 compared to the ₱1,056,000.00 appropriation as per GAD plan. Due to the unforeseen increases of honorarium and number of Teachers who manned the Day Care Centers, the appropriated amounts for their honorarium were depleted. The Appropriation Ordinance which enacted the Annual Budget provided under Section 4-B provides that the Local Chief Executive was authorized to issue Executive Order to augment any item in the approved Annual Budget for their respective offices from savings in other items within the same expense class of their respective appropriation. With this provision provided under the Appropriation Ordinance the Local Chief Executive issued Executive Order No. 76, series of 2020, dated December 29, 2020, Management augment the lacking amount of ₱110,080.33 to the budgeted amount of ₱1,420,104.00 to complete the needed amount of ₱1,530,184.33.
Management further explained that the GAD plan was prepared one (1) year ahead before the preparation of the Annual Budget in order to give ample time for the DILG to review the plan. When the Annual Budget for CY 2020 was prepared the appropriations for Day Care Services were raised from ₱1,056,000.00 to ₱1,420,104.00. The same with the Operation of Stimulation and Therapeutic Activity Center which was raised from ₱240,000.00 to ₱300,000.00 in order to meet the 5% minimum Gender Budget. The said increases were no longer incorporated in the GAD plan submitted to the DILG since it was prepared earlier than the Annual Budget. Since the approved   appropriation for Operation of STAC (Breaking Barriers for Disabled Children) PPAs was ₱300,000.00 the utilization had not exceeded the appropriation, instead it has an outstanding balance of ₱16,874.00 as of December 31, 2020.
While we do not contest the justification of Management, we sustain, however, that expenditures in the total amount of ₱3,120,250.80 were unrelated to GAD, hence, not in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA. The Audit Team may consider all the listed four (4) items to be compliant with the aforesaid Joint Circular provided that “they are justified as clearly addressing a specific gender issue.”
We also suggest that the LGU submit revised or updated GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG in case there are changes to be made in the previously submitted GAD Plan and Budget. Section 6.0 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 provides, in part, that: “The LGUs through their LCEs shall inform the concerned DILG Office in writing if there are changes in the DILG endorsed GPB and/or if there is a need to implement additional PPAs relevant to current gender issues or GAD related undertakings. The concerned DILG Office, in turn, shall acknowledge receipt of the adjusted GPB and shall inform the LGU if the GAD PPAs or activities in the adjusted GPB are in accordance with the JMC, as amended.”

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairman of the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC), implement programs, projects and activities (PPAs) under LCPC in accordance with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 to address the needs of children and other related issues that need immediate attention and action.
We also recommended that members of the LCPC maximize the allocated LCPC Fund to identified PPAs for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries to fully achieve the objectives of the law creating the fund.
Management commented that the charging of the above expenditures to the LCPC fund is appropriate since the target beneficiaries were intended for the protection of children with the aim to address the needs of children especially those with special needs due to various kinds of disability/impairment that requires immediate attention and action.
Management explained that among the fundamental rights of children such as Survival, Development and Participation, Protection rights cover those which guard the children against all forms of abuses, exploitation and discrimination. Children with disability were considered in extreme difficult circumstances to be victims of neglect from their irresponsible parents and discrimination from other children with no disability. Management believes that the services and activities offered and implemented through the operation of Stimulation and Therapeutic Activity Center (STAC) of LGU Manaoag were suitable to the expenditures charged to the LCPC fund.
Management further explained that the rest of the expenditures were mainstreamed/attributed to the items budgeted under the MSWD Office, RHU, Mayor’s Office and other Offices which were collated to form the LCPC budget and was incorporated to the approved Annual Investment Plan (AIP). Since the AIP was approved, Management assumed that all the items incorporated therein were in accordance with the guidelines in preparation of the LCPC Plan.

Management informed the Audit Team that the Municipal Treasurer already transferred to General Fund Account the balance of ₱750,000.00 of LDRRMF for Capital Outlay for FY 2021.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MPDC, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that prior year’s unexpended balance of LDRRMF is included in the revised Annual Investment Program (AIP) duly approved by the local Sanggunian and Local Chief Executive which shall serve as the basis in the utilization thereof.
Management expressed its intention to adhere to the recommendation and include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Annual Investment Program (AIP).
We recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the existing rules and regulations concerning the remittance of taxes withheld.
We recommended that the local government unit continue to comply with the GSIS regulations remittance of monthly personal share of employees and the contribution for employer’s share.
We recommended that the local government unit continue to adhere with the existing rules and regulations concerning the remittance of Pag-IBIG premiums.
We recommended that the local government unit continue to adhere with the existing rules and regulations concerning the remittance of PhilHealth premiums.
